Open to research & engineering opportunities

Hi, I'm Eslam Software Engineer | Researcher | Instructor

I build things that run close to the hardware and think at the edge. Specializing in Embedded Linux, autonomous systems, and IoT — I bridge deep technical engineering with hands-on teaching for the next generation of engineers.

Embedded Linux Federated Learning Autonomous Vehicles FreeRTOS / ARM PyTorch IoT / MQTT Yocto / Buildroot

expertise

What I Build & Teach

From bare-metal microcontrollers to distributed ML systems — a full-stack embedded engineer.

Embedded Linux

Kernel development, device drivers, BSPs, and custom Linux distributions with Yocto and Buildroot.

Yocto Buildroot Kernel Dev Device Drivers

Microcontrollers & RTOS

ARM Cortex-M, AVR, FreeRTOS task scheduling, interrupt handling, and bare-metal systems programming.

ARM Cortex-M AVR FreeRTOS Assembly

Machine Learning Research

Two-stream CNN for steering prediction, federated learning, optical flow methods, and model compression.

PyTorch CNN Federated Learning OpenCV

IoT Systems

ESP32 firmware, MQTT messaging, Node-RED dashboards, LoRa/NB-IoT for Egyptian networks, TinyML.

MQTT ESP32 Node-RED LoRa

Systems Programming

C/C++ systems development, Qt framework for embedded UIs, Linux IPC, and POSIX APIs.

C/C++ Qt POSIX Linux IPC

Teaching & Curriculum

500+ students trained across Zewail City, ITI, IMT School, and Amit Learning in embedded, IoT, and C++.

Embedded Systems IoT C++ Training Lab Design
roadmap

Embedded Linux Roadmap

A structured path from C fundamentals to production-grade embedded Linux systems — built from 8+ years of teaching and engineering.

01

C & Linux Basics

Pointers, memory, Makefiles, POSIX APIs, and the Linux command line.

03

Bootloaders

Boot sequence, U-Boot configuration, Device Tree, and board porting.

05

Device Drivers

Kernel modules, character drivers, platform drivers, I2C/SPI/UART.

07

Yocto & Buildroot

Custom distros, layers, recipes, BitBake, and SDK generation.

View Full Roadmap

Embedded Linux Diploma — Lab 03: Writing Your First Kernel Module

Hands-on guide to writing, compiling, and loading Linux kernel modules. Covers module lifecycle, parameters, proc filesystem, and basic character drivers.

linux Feb 01, 2024

Embedded Linux Diploma — Lab 02: Linux Boot Process & U-Boot

Understanding the full Linux boot sequence: from power-on reset through U-Boot, kernel decompression, and init system startup.
